name: lattice
description: Composable AI skills framework covering the full software delivery lifecycle — design, architecture, implementation, testing, and review.

interface.shortDescription: Composable SDLC skills for design, architecture, implementation, testing, and review.
interface.longDescription: Lattice gives Codex eight structured molecules that cover the full delivery lifecycle: Requirement Forge (collaborative product interview → structured specs), Design Blueprint (five-level design process from capabilities to contracts), Code Forge (inside-out implementation with architecture and security guardrails), Review (severity-ordered code review across quality, architecture, DDD, and security), Bug Fix (reproduce-first repair with regression protection), Refactor Safely (characterization-first restructuring without breaking behavior), Architecture Compass (codebase assessment with recommended direction), and Lattice Init (guided project setup). Each molecule composes atoms — architecture, clean code, DDD, secure coding, test quality — loaded selectively based on what your code touches.
defaultPrompt: Forge requirements for a new feature.
Create a design blueprint for this feature.
Implement from an approved design blueprint.
Review this code for quality, architecture, and security.
Fix this bug — investigate, reproduce, and patch it safely.
Refactor this code safely without breaking behavior.
Assess my codebase architecture and recommend direction.
Set up Lattice for this project.
